title: lagascea heteropappus
intro: Lagascea heteropappus, also known as Mexican Feathergrass or Golden Buttons, is a flowering plant native to Mexico and the southwestern United States. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antipyretic properties, which help to reduce pain and fever, and promote healing in wounds. The plant is rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, compounds that have been shown to have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects, providing health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving immune function. Lagascea heteropappus can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions.
